message.content:
The phrase "Just say hello" carries different significances across various contexts, as highlighted in the search results:

1. **LA Civil Rights Campaign** – The [Los Angeles Civil + Human Rights and Equity Department's #LAForAll campaign](https://civilandhumanrights.lacity.gov/campaigns/justsayhello) uses "Just Say Hello" as a hate crime prevention initiative. It encourages residents to greet each other in 14 languages (e.g., *Hola*, *Annyeonghaseyo*, *Kumusta*) to foster belonging, break down barriers, and build a more inclusive city. The campaign appears on city assets like sanitation vehicles and LAX billboards.

2. **Social Connection Service** – [Just Say Hello.com](https://www.justsayhello.com/) is a service offering personalized phone calls to combat loneliness and social isolation, particularly targeting seniors, youth, and young adults. It emphasizes that a simple greeting can improve mental well-being and create supportive communities.

3. **Cultural Reference** – The phrase is also the title of a song by Melo D, with lyrics about longing and connection, available on platforms like YouTube.

In essence, "Just say hello" serves as both a symbolic call for inclusivity and a practical tool for fostering human connection.

url_citation.title: Just Say Hello. Hola. Annyeonghaseyo. Kumusta.
url_citation.content:
Just Say Hello. Hola. Annyeonghaseyo. Kumusta. | Civil + Human Rights and Equity Department Skip to main content

# Just Say Hello. Hola. Annyeonghaseyo. Kumusta.

A New Chapter of Our Award-Winning [#LAForAll Campaign] 

Los Angeles is a city of millions, speaking hundreds of languages and representing countless communities from the mountains to the sea. But no matter our differences, we all call this city home.

As the next phase of our award-winning #LAForAll campaign, Just Say Hello continues the work of LA Civil Rights to advance belonging, inclusion, and anti-discrimination across our city. Designed by Masaki Koike, this campaign lives at the intersection of art, language, advocacy, and community. It empowers every Angeleno to make Los Angeles more resilient, more inclusive, and more united.

This hate crime prevention campaign starts with one word: Hello.

The campaign is available in the following 14 languages:

- English: Hello
- Armenian: բարև (Barev)
- Arabic: مرحبًا (Marhaba)
- Hebrew: שלום (Shalom)
- Vietnamese: Xin chào
- Cambodian (Khmer): សួស្តី (Suŏ sdei)
- Thai: สวัสดี (S̄wạs̄dī)
- Japanese: こんにちは (Konnichiwa)
- Italian: Ciao
- Croatian: Pozdrav zdravo
- Spanish: Hola
- Korean: 안녕하세요 (Annyeonghaseyo)
- Simplified and Traditional Chinese: 你好 (Nǐ hǎo)
- Russian: Привет (Privet)

A simple greeting has the power to break down invisible barriers, spark connection, and remind us that we belong to something greater than ourselves. Every "hello" helps build a city rooted in kindness, community, and shared humanity—because hate has no home in Los Angeles.

LA is For Everyone. So, Just Say Hello to your fellow Angeleno.

Break barriers. Build bridges.

We extend our sincere gratitude to the Weingart Foundation, Port of Los Angeles, and the Los Angeles Public Library for funding this hate crime prevention campaign. We also thankful for the support and opportunity to collaborate on this campaign with our partner departments, Los Angeles World Airports, LA Recreation and Parks, and LA Sanitation.

## Discover Just Say Hello!

Just Say Hello Citywide Launch

In Recognition of International Friendship Day 2025LA Civil Rights is proud to launch the citywide Just Say Hello campaign — now available in 11 languages.

This powerful initiative will soon be featured on LA Sanitation vehicles, digital billboards at every LAX terminal, digital bus shelters, and more across Los Angeles.

Join the movement to celebrate culture and connection — and help build an LA that’s truly for everyone, one friendly greeting at a time.

#JustSayHelloLA | Break Barriers. Build Bridges.

Above: Mayor Karen Bass, City leaders, and staff proudly display Just Say Hello campaign posters and bumper stickers — celebrating connection, culture, and a friendlier Los Angeles.

Just Say Hello Launch

At the Lunar New Year Festival, LA Civil Rights will share materials at our booth and encourage attendees to follow us on social media for our Just Say Hello digital challenge.

Join us for a day of celebration, connection, and culture—then, be part of the excitement at 6:00 PM as we launch our 'Just Say Hello' campaign!

Just Say Hello Selfie Submission

Submit a photograph of yourself and/or your child as part of our Just Say Hello Campaign! Sign the waiver and upload your photo to receive a custom Just Say Hello picture to post to social media using the hashtag #JustSayHelloLA.

Show your support for the #JustSayHelloLA campaign - Submit your Selfie!

url_citation.title: Personalized Phone Calls | Just Say Hello.com LLC
url_citation.content:
# Just Say Hello.Com

# Transforming Lives, One Call at a Time

At Just Say Hello.com, we believe in the power of a simple greeting. We deeply understand the impact of loneliness and social isolation. That's why we offer personalized phone calls designed to brighten someone's day, create a sense of belonging, and nurture strong, supportive communities. Each heartfelt "hello" we share is more than just a general greeting, it’s a step toward better mental well-being and a more compassionate society.  We invite you to join us in making the world a kinder, more connected place where everyone feels seen, heard, and valued.

[Sign Up Now] 

[Learn More] 

Connecting Generations

## Senior Engagement Services

### Compassionate Check-Ins

Our friendly calls provide a vital lifeline for seniors, reducing feelings of loneliness and enhancing their overall well-being.

[Learn More] 

### Building Relationships

We create meaningful connections through regular, personalized interactions that foster a sense of belonging and community.

[Get Started] 

### Peace of Mind for Families

Families can rest easy knowing their loved ones are receiving regular, caring check-ins that prioritize their mental health.

[Discover Our Services] 

### Enhancing Quality of Life

Our mission-driven services aim to improve the quality of life for seniors, combating social isolation one call at a time.

[Join Us Today] 

Empowering Youth

## Youth and Adolescence Services

### Supportive Connections

Navigating the challenges of youth can be tough. Our services provide a compassionate ear for children and teens, helping them build resilience and foster connections during these formative years.
[Learn More] 

### Combatting Bullying

With 1 in 5 students affected by bullying and social exclusion, we offer tailored support to help them overcome these obstacles and thrive academically and emotionally.

[Get Support] 

### Building Resilience

Our dedicated team is here to empower youth, providing them with the tools and support they need to navigate their social landscape with confidence.

[Contact Us] 

### Creating Connections

We believe in the power of connection. Our personalized phone calls help youth feel heard and valued, reducing feelings of loneliness and isolation.

[Connect Now] 

Empowering Connections

## Young Adult Services

### Navigating Independence

College life can be overwhelming. With over 60% of students reporting anxiety and 40% feeling isolated, our personalized calls offer a supportive space to share experiences and find encouragement.
[Learn More] 

### A Safe Space

Our compassionate professionals are here to listen and help you adjust to this pivotal stage of life. Together, we can tackle the challenges of independence and foster a sense of belonging.

[Sign up] 

### Thrive Together

Join a community that understands your journey. Our tailored phone call programs are designed to create meaningful interactions that combat loneliness and enhance mental well-being.

[Contact Us] 

### Your Voice Matters

Every call is an opportunity to connect, share, and grow. Let us help you navigate this transition with personalized support that makes a difference.

[Get help] 

Connecting Families, One Call at a Time

## Busy Family Services

### Support for Single Parents

Navigating parenthood can be overwhelming, especially for single parents. Our calls provide a comforting voice, offering empathy and understanding during those challenging moments.
[Learn More] 

### A Moment of Connection

Every call is a reminder that you’re not alone. We’re here to listen, share, and uplift, helping to ease the loneliness that often accompanies parenting.

[Join Us] 

### Empathy and Reassurance

Our compassionate professionals are dedicated to providing support tailored to your unique journey, ensuring you feel valued and understood.

[Contact Us] 

### Building Stronger Connections

Through our personalized calls, we aim to strengthen the bonds within families, fostering a sense of belonging and community for all parents.

[Connect Now] 

Reconnect with Yourself

## Self-Engagement Services

### A Moment for You

In the hustle of daily life, it's easy to forget about your own needs. Our self-engagement services offer a compassionate voice to remind you that self-care is essential.
[Learn More] 

### Recharge Your Spirit

Take a break from your responsibilities and enjoy a moment of connection. Our calls provide a safe space to share, reflect, and rejuvenate your spirit.

[Sign Up] 

### Embrace Self-Care

Loneliness can creep in, but you don’t have to face it alone. Our dedicated team is here to support you with personalized calls that prioritize your well-being.
[Get Started] 

### Contact Us

Join a network of individuals who understand the importance of connection. Our services foster a sense of belonging and help you feel valued and heard.
[Join Us] 

## Veteran Engagement Services

You served.  You sacrificed.  Now you are alone.  The good news is that you don't have to be.

#### You served.

Even while actively serving, many Veterans experience loneliness and isolation despite being surrounded by fellow service members. The demands of military life, long deployments, and separation from loved ones can create emotional distance and a sense of disconnection.

[Learn more] 

#### You sacrificed.

Service members may struggle to find meaningful personal connections as they focus on their duties, often suppressing emotions to maintain resilience. This environment, while fostering discipline and strength, can leave individuals feeling as though they must navigate personal challenges alone.

﻿

[Contact Us] 

#### Now you are Alone.

The camaraderie of the military provides support, but the absence of close family and civilian relationships can lead to moments of profound loneliness, making the transition to civilian life even more challenging.

﻿

[Sign Up] 

###### QUICK LINKS

###### SERVICES

###### Senior Engagement Services

###### Youth and Adolescence Services

###### Young Adults Services

###### Single Parents Services

###### Self-Engagement Services

###### Veterans Engagement Services

###### Employees Assistance Program Services

###### AI Business Process Automation Services

###### CONTACT

###### (844) 380-4441

###### info@justsayhello.com

###### P.O. Box 44556

###### Atlanta, GA  30311

###### Copyright JustSayHello.com 2025

url_citation.title: 35 Other Ways to Say “Hello” (With Examples) - grammarwaves.com
url_citation.content:
35 Other Ways to Say “Hello” (With Examples) - grammarwaves.com

When it comes to communication, the simple word “hello” is often the first impression we make. It signals greeting, openness, friendliness, and connection. However, using the same greeting repeatedly can feel stale or impersonal, especially in professional emails, text messages, phone calls, or social settings.

```
Choosing alternative ways to say hello allows you to express warmth, enthusiasm, or politeness, depending on context. Whether connecting with a colleague, reaching out to a friend, or introducing yourself to someone new, using varied greetings enhances your tone, personality, and relationship-building skills.
```

This guide presents 35 [alternative] ways to say hello, complete with meanings, explanations, examples, best and worst uses, and tone guidance. These expressions ensure your greetings are fresh, engaging, and contextually appropriate, whether formal, casual, or professional.

## What Does “Hello” Mean?

The word “hello” is a greeting used to acknowledge, welcome, or initiate conversation. It signals openness, friendliness, and engagement. While universally recognized, the tone, setting, and phrasing of a greeting can drastically affect how the message is received.

## When to Use “Hello”

Use hello or its alternatives when:

- Initiating conversation in casual or formal settings
- Greeting friends, family, or colleagues
- Sending professional or personal emails
- Answering or starting a phone call
- Meeting someone in person for the first time

## Is It Professional/Polite to Say “Hello”?

Yes, “hello” is both professional and polite. In formal or workplace situations, alternatives like “good morning,” “greetings,” or “hi there” may sound more refined. Selecting the appropriate greeting ensures you communicate respect, warmth, and attentiveness.

## Pros and Cons

### Pros

- Polite and approachable
- Quick and simple way to start conversation
- Universally understood

### Cons

- Lacks enthusiasm without additional context
- Overuse may reduce impact
- Can feel generic or impersonal

## Other Ways to Say Hello

Here are 35 other [ways to say] “hello”:

- Hi mate
- Greetings and salutations
- Ciao
- Bonjour
- Hola
- How do you do
- Ahoy
- Yo there
- Hey friend
- Hey buddy
- Evening
- Morning
- Top of the morning
- Long time no see
- Nice to see you
- Pleasure to meet you
- How’s it going
- Hey all
- Hey everyone
- Hi all
- Hi everyone
- Salutations
- Welcome
- Yo
- Hiya
- What’s up
- Howdy
- Hey there
- Good evening
- Good afternoon
- Good morning
- Greetings
- Hi there
- Hello there
- Hey
- Hi

## Hi

Meaning: Simple and friendly greeting.

Explanation: Casual and universally recognized; suitable for almost all informal contexts.

Example: “Hi, how are you doing today?”

Best Use: Friends, colleagues, casual interactions.

Worst Use: Formal introductions or professional emails without context.

Tone: Friendly, approachable, neutral

## Hey

Meaning: Casual and informal greeting.

Explanation: Conveys friendliness and sometimes excitement; best for close acquaintances.

Example: “Hey! Long time no see.”

Best Use: Friends, family, informal settings.

Worst Use: Professional or formal introductions.

Tone: Casual, warm, enthusiastic

## Hello There

Meaning: Warm and slightly playful greeting.

Explanation: Adds personality to a traditional hello; can signal friendliness.

Example: “Hello there! It’s great to see you.”

Best Use: Social gatherings, informal emails, conversations.

Worst Use: Highly formal contexts.

Tone: Friendly, warm, inviting

## Hi There

Meaning: Polite and casual greeting.

Explanation: Adds slight formality compared to hey, yet still approachable.

Example: “Hi there! Can we talk about the project?”

[READ MORE... 35 Other Ways to Say ‘For the First Time’ (With Examples)] 

Best Use: Semi-formal emails, casual conversations.

Worst Use: Strictly professional or formal letters.

Tone: Friendly, professional-casual

## Greetings

Meaning: Formal and respectful salutation.

Explanation: Often used in writing, emails, or speeches.

Example: “Greetings, I hope this message finds you well.”

Best Use: Formal correspondence, letters, presentations.

Worst Use: Casual text messages.

Tone: Formal, polite, professional

## Good Morning

Meaning: Time-specific greeting expressing politeness.

Explanation: Signals respect and awareness of context.

Example: “Good morning! How was your weekend?”

Best Use: Workplace, professional settings, first encounters of the day.

Worst Use: Evening meetings or casual messages outside the morning.

Tone: Polite, professional, respectful

## Good Afternoon

Meaning: Polite greeting used after midday.

Explanation: Time-aware and professional; often used in workplaces or formal emails.

Example: “Good afternoon, everyone. Let’s start the meeting.”

Best Use: Work emails, meetings, professional interactions.

Worst Use: Early morning or casual chats.

Tone: Professional, courteous

## Good Evening

Meaning: Time-appropriate, polite greeting for late-day encounters.

Explanation: Often used for formal dinners, events, or evening emails.

Example: “Good evening, I hope your day went well.”

Best Use: Professional or formal evening settings.

Worst Use: Casual morning conversations.

Tone: Formal, polite, respectful

## Hey There

Meaning: Friendly and approachable greeting.

Explanation: Combines casual tone with warmth; less formal than hello there.

Example: “Hey there! How’s your day going?”

Best Use: Friends, casual emails, informal meetings.

Worst Use: Formal business introductions.

Tone: Casual, warm, friendly

## Howdy

Meaning: Informal, regional greeting.

Explanation: Commonly associated with southern or western English-speaking regions; casual and cheerful.

Example: “Howdy! Ready for the weekend?”

Best Use: Casual, friendly conversations.

Worst Use: Professional or formal emails.

Tone: Friendly, relaxed, regional

## What’s Up

Meaning: Casual, informal greeting asking about current activities or mood.

Explanation: Often paired with friendliness and humor.

Example: “What’s up? Haven’t seen you in a while.”

Best Use: Friends, texting, informal emails.

Worst Use: Formal introductions or professional settings.

Tone: Casual, cheerful, approachable

## Hiya

Meaning: Friendly and playful greeting.

Explanation: A casual, cheerful way to say hello, often used in informal conversations or texting.

Example: “Hiya! Long time no chat.”

Best Use: Friends, casual texting, informal meetings.

Worst Use: Formal emails or professional settings.

Tone: Cheerful, informal, friendly

## Yo

Meaning: Very casual and urban greeting.

Explanation: Often signals camaraderie or informal acknowledgment.

Example: “Yo! How’s it going?”

Best Use: Friends, peers, casual social situations.

Worst Use: Professional or formal introductions.

Tone: Casual, energetic, friendly

## Welcome

Meaning: Greeting with warmth and hospitality.

Explanation: Expresses inclusion and friendliness when someone arrives.

Example: “Welcome! It’s great to have you here.”

Best Use: Events, new team members, visitors.

Worst Use: Brief casual interactions.

Tone: Warm, inviting, friendly

## Salutations

Meaning: Formal and classic greeting.

Explanation: Often used in written correspondence or formal speeches.

Example: “Salutations, esteemed colleagues.”

Best Use: Letters, formal emails, presentations.

Worst Use: Casual texting or conversations.

Tone: Formal, professional, polite

## Hi Everyone

Meaning: Inclusive greeting for a group.

Explanation: Used when addressing multiple people at once.

Example: “Hi everyone, welcome to the meeting.”

Best Use: Team emails, meetings, group chats.

Worst Use: One-on-one conversations.

[READ MORE... 35 Other Ways to Say “Just So You Know” (With Examples)] 

Tone: Friendly, professional, inclusive

## Hi All

Meaning: Similar to “Hi Everyone,” slightly more casual.

Explanation: Acknowledges the presence of a group in a polite but informal way.

Example: “Hi all, please see the updates below.”

Best Use: Group emails, team chats.

Worst Use: Formal letters.

Tone: Friendly, professional-casual

## Hey Everyone

Meaning: Informal greeting for a group.

Explanation: Adds casual, energetic tone to addressing a team or friends.

Example: “Hey everyone! Ready for the workshop?”

Best Use: Team meetings, casual group texts.

Worst Use: Formal communication.

Tone: Casual, warm, approachable

## Hey All

Meaning: Casual, group-friendly greeting.

Explanation: Similar to “Hey Everyone,” slightly shorter and informal.

Example: “Hey all, let’s start the session.”

Best Use: Friends, informal team chats.

Worst Use: Professional emails or formal documents.

Tone: Casual, friendly

## How’s It Going

Meaning: Casual greeting asking about someone’s current state.

Explanation: Often paired with friendliness or small talk.

Example: “How’s it going? Haven’t seen you in a while.”

Best Use: Friends, colleagues in informal contexts.

Worst Use: Formal introductions or emails.

Tone: Friendly, conversational, informal

## Pleasure to Meet You

Meaning: Polite, formal introduction greeting.

Explanation: Used when meeting someone for the first time; conveys respect and positivity.

Example: “Pleasure to meet you. I’ve heard great things about your work.”

Best Use: Professional networking, formal events.

Worst Use: Casual texting.

Tone: Formal, respectful, positive

## Nice to See You

Meaning: Friendly acknowledgment when seeing someone again.

Explanation: Expresses warmth and recognition, often after a period apart.

Example: “Nice to see you! How have you been?”

Best Use: Personal or professional encounters.

Worst Use: First-time introductions.

Tone: Friendly, warm, approachable

## Long Time No See

Meaning: Acknowledges absence and reconnects.

Explanation: Used informally to greet someone after a while.

Example: “Long time no see! How have things been?”

Best Use: Friends, casual acquaintances.

Worst Use: Formal business emails.

Tone: Casual, friendly, nostalgic

## Top of the Morning

Meaning: Time-specific, cheerful greeting.

Explanation: Classic, friendly phrase, often associated with Irish English.

Example: “Top of the morning! Ready for the day?”

Best Use: Informal mornings, friendly chats.

Worst Use: Professional or evening settings.

Tone: Cheerful, informal, energetic

## Morning

Meaning: Short, casual morning greeting.

Explanation: Simple acknowledgment at the start of the day.

Example: “Morning! How’s your day starting?”

Best Use: Colleagues, friends, casual encounters.

Worst Use: Formal meetings where full “Good morning” is preferred.

Tone: Friendly, neutral, casual

## Evening

Meaning: Time-appropriate, brief greeting.

Explanation: Acknowledges the time of day in casual conversation.

Example: “Evening! How was your day?”

Best Use: Friends, casual interactions.

Worst Use: Formal email openings.

Tone: Casual, neutral

## Hey Buddy

Meaning: Friendly greeting to a close acquaintance.

Explanation: Adds warmth and familiarity.

Example: “Hey buddy! Ready for the game?”

Best Use: Friends, peers.

Worst Use: Professional or formal contexts.

Tone: Casual, warm, personal

## Hey Friend

Meaning: Inclusive and friendly greeting.

Explanation: Polite and warm, but casual enough for relaxed conversations.

Example: “Hey friend! How have you been?”

Best Use: Friends, informal messaging.

Worst Use: Professional communication.

Tone: Friendly, approachable

## Yo There

Meaning: Casual, energetic greeting.

Explanation: Adds emphasis and friendliness, often used among peers.

[READ MORE... 35 Other Ways to Say “Excited to See You” (With Examples)] 

Example: “Yo there! Long time no talk.”

Best Use: Informal, social interactions.

Worst Use: Formal or professional settings.

Tone: Energetic, casual, informal

## Ahoy

Meaning: Playful and fun greeting.

Explanation: Originally nautical, now used humorously or in creative contexts.

Example: “Ahoy! Welcome aboard the project team.”

Best Use: Friendly, playful introductions.

Worst Use: Serious professional meetings.

Tone: Playful, informal, unique

## How Do You Do

Meaning: Formal and polite greeting.

Explanation: Traditional phrase used in professional or diplomatic contexts.

Example: “How do you do? I’m pleased to meet you.”

Best Use: Formal introductions, networking.

Worst Use: Casual conversation or texting.

Tone: Formal, respectful

## Hola

Meaning: Spanish greeting for “hello.”

Explanation: Widely recognized internationally, adds cultural flair.

Example: “Hola! ¿Cómo estás?”

Best Use: Multilingual contexts, informal greetings.

Worst Use: Formal English-only communication.

Tone: Friendly, casual, international

## Bonjour

Meaning: French greeting for “good day” or “hello.”

Explanation: Adds elegance and cultural touch.

Example: “Bonjour! Comment ça va?”

Best Use: Formal, international, or friendly encounters.

Worst Use: Casual English-only texting.

Tone: Polite, elegant, international

## Ciao

Meaning: Italian greeting meaning “hello” or “goodbye.”

Explanation: Casual and stylish greeting, widely recognized.

Example: “Ciao! Great to see you.”

Best Use: Informal social encounters.

Worst Use: Formal or professional emails.

Tone: Casual, friendly, stylish

## Greetings and Salutations

Meaning: Formal, comprehensive greeting.

Explanation: Often used in writing or ceremonial contexts.

Example: “Greetings and salutations! Welcome to the event.”

Best Use: Letters, speeches, professional contexts.

Worst Use: Casual texting.

Tone: Formal, polished, professional

## Hi Mate

Meaning: Friendly, regional greeting (British/Australian English).

Explanation: Adds casual friendliness and cultural flair.

Example: “Hi mate! How’s your weekend going?”

Best Use: Friends, informal contexts.

Worst Use: Formal professional communication.

Tone: Friendly, casual, informal

## Final Thoughts

Greeting someone is more than a simple word; it is a way to express friendliness, politeness, and emotional connection. Using varied greetings ensures your interactions feel personal, warm, and contextually appropriate. From casual friends to professional colleagues, selecting the right greeting sets the tone for the conversation. By using these 35 other ways to say hello, you can convey enthusiasm, respect, approachability, or cultural awareness depending on context. Thoughtful greetings improve relationships, create positive first impressions, and make communication more engaging across personal, social, and professional settings.

## FAQs

### Is it okay to use casual greetings at work?

Yes, in informal office settings casual greetings like hi or hey are acceptable, but use good morning or hello there for formal emails or meetings.

### How do I greet a group of people professionally?

Use greetings like hi everyone, good morning team, or greetings to include everyone politely.

### Can I use foreign greetings like “hola” or “bonjour”?

Yes, especially in multicultural or international contexts, as long as your audience understands or appreciates it.

### What’s the difference between “hi” and “hey”?

Hi is neutral and friendly; hey is more casual and informal, often used with friends or peers.

### How do I make my greeting more enthusiastic?

Add warmth, context, or time-specific phrases like “good morning”, “hello there!”, or playful expressions like “ahoy”.
